summary: Strong Terrapin Station and Space jam with a beautiful Brokedown Palace
blurb: The show delivers a powerful Terrapin Station and an inspired post‑drums Space segment, while the encore closes on a gorgeous Brokedown Palace. Jerry’s guitar shines on several numbers though his vocals draw mixed reactions, and Brent’s keyboard tone sits forward in the mix.
review: On March 31 1983 at the Warfield, the Dead rolled out a set that balances tight songcraft with the improvisational daring fans love. The first set opens fire with “Feel Like A Stranger” and rolls through classics like “Friend Of The Devil,” while “My Brother Esau” stands out as a highlight of the night. The second set is where the magic really builds – a seamless “Lost Sailor > Saint Of Circumstance” launch into a sprawling “Terrapin Station,” followed by a thunderous drum duet that blossoms into an exhilarating post‑drums “Space.” That jam segues into a riveting “The Other One” and keeps the momentum through “Throwing Stones,” “Wharf Rat,” and a rollicking “Good Lovin’.” The night ends on a heartfelt note with a beautifully rendered “Brokedown Palace.” Jerry’s lead work is at times incandescent, especially on “Terrapin Station” and the Space jam, though his vocal delivery sparked mixed opinions among listeners. Brent’s keyboard tone is rich but occasionally pushes forward in the mix, while Bob provides strong vocal contributions throughout. Overall, the performance is solid, with a few rough edges that don’t detract from its lasting appeal.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three of the four recordings are described as positive, praising Terrapin Station, Space jam, Brother Esau, Lost Sailor > Saint Of Circumstance and Brokedown Palace. Only one recording is mixed, noting uneven Garcia vocals but still highlighting strong moments. The preponderance of upbeat language (“solid,” “inspired,” “high‑quality”) warrants a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crisp, audiophile‑grade clarity; it presents the full show with minimal flaws and showcases the highlighted songs in the best possible fidelity.

key_highlights: Terrapin Station delivers a powerful performance and strong guitar work.
Post‑drums Space jam is an inspired improvisational peak.
Brother Esau (My Brother Esau) stands out as a memorable moment.
Encore closes with a beautiful Brokedown Palace.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar shines on Terrapin Station, Space and the encore; his vocals receive mixed feedback, with some listeners calling them sloppy while others note moments of brilliance.
band_performance.Bob: Bob’s vocal contributions dominate several numbers and are praised as highlights; one reviewer notes a missing guitar part in the mix.
band_performance.Brent: Brent’s keyboard tone is rich and full, though a few listeners find it slightly loud in the mix on certain songs.
